item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at "Roll Out event" +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is truly terrifying. Whoever made this, great job! 👍

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
body horror at its finest

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
uugghhnhhh very gross… and I can imagine the transformations smell too :-/

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: body horror done right, damn. i do have one nitpick: calling it a "roll-out event" is not very formal/scientific. i'd change that name. other than that, pretty neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instant upvote after that last test.

item.title: Re: How do you add the cool banner thing at the top with item # and object class?
item.content:encoded: This article uses this component here by Woedenaz.

item.title: Re: How do you add the cool banner thing at the top with item # and object class?
item.content:encoded: It's custom formatting. You can check page source under options (in same bar as discuss) and copy and paste or make one (idk the process though).

item.title: How do you add the cool banner thing at the top with item # and object class?
item.content:encoded: How?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dear lord why must you remind me of that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a lifelong Transformers fan, this was definitely not the delightful surprise I would have expected on a Wednesday morning. This combines my favourite aspect of horror with my favourite franchise of all time, and I have to wonder how the hell I hadn't come up with this earlier. Great job on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree; this is one of the best recent articles I have read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“Roll out event” is just too silly of a name for real scientists to be using, I really don’t think it fits. If this was a -J then sure use funny names. But in a real article the funny has to be nuanced and realistic not in your face. And the last note definitely doesn’t hit right for me, if they’re lower on the totem pole than the head researcher they wouldn’t ask the biology question and if they aren’t then he definitely would explain it. Your jokes just aren’t in the right place, the foundation personnel aren’t the source of jokes yet every source for your humor comes from their unrealistic dialogue

Dado articles are handled really well by most authors and you should read some before trying for a funny approach. All descriptions used are realistic to a scientist while yours just aren’t. the terms are too silly

The article itself is really well written and definitely an interesting take but I really would like to see the serious scientists be serious on here. A lot of 5000 articles are starting to write researchers as 16 year olds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, especially for jetpack Sarkicists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fucking love this as a transformers fan. As soon as I saw the cover image, I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, and moreover yes. Exploit my childhood to your heart's content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
jesus christ this is disgusting +1

Am I the only one reminded of Shed 17?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not what I was expecting from a Sarkic scp +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god the pun here

I was hoping someone would take the "more than meets the eye" theme and do a fucked-up take on Transformers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, and not just because if I had entered jamcon this is probably exactly what I would have written too. Lots of fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roll Out
Rolled Out

I enjoy "things that do a thing" SCPs way too much; When I read this I felt a level of enjoyment unimaginable. I originally thought this was going to be about a bike made of bacon, but this is was much better than I thought it would be.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HammerMaiden, instead of posting multiple short comments, edit your previous posts using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

badum tss
badjoke.mp4

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this for the description and the first two test logs, however by the third one it felt like it was starting to slip into torture porn more than serving any descriptive purpose. I think cutting the second test log entirely, and dialing back the rest just a tad would make this a more palatable experience without compromising the horror that works.

Everything here is working phenomenally well but the testing logs take just a bit too much time wallowing in how awful this effect is. While that's delivering on what the description promised, there's a certain portion of it that just feels like horribleness for its own sake and not developing what's already been established. That's a fine line you have to walk in an SCP like this, and this does it for the most part, but you're going to have to dial it back there about 20%.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe I just replied instead of editing, omg.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ughf. Disgoostang.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do love me some black comedy SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed your tags, the video tag is for article with videos in them. media is what you were looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 from me and this is disgusting my inner child.

Like I wanted to be a transformer when I was a little kid, like "Oh I wanna be like Ratchet!" and make the noises… this is both nostalgic and disgusting my inner child on a whole new scale.

Conprocs eh their alright. Do love the HMCL being there.

Description: Hmmmm I love it! Like, again, this has made me rethink on being a transformer when I was a kid (See above.)

Experiment logs… Damn, that's some powerful imagery. Also I really want to know how the human rocket got to the moon and where did the fuel come from?

All in all: +1 from me. PS Sarkic Jetpacks when?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-5059-1c's heart was ejected from the main cannon at a rate of 257 m/s." Beautiful imagery.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gruesomely vivid and delightfully uncomfortable.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
TRANSFORMERS - ROBOTS IN DISGUISE

This is a tribute to 80's nostalgia, and in some ways SCP wiki nostalgia as well. Ever re-watch a film from your childhood and just, really wanna live that drama, have those powers, go on those adventures?

Well… maybe if you're lucky your dream will come true.

Image is taken right off of Flicker under CC By 2.0. You can find it here.

Many people to thank. The usual folks had eyes on this over time, Cerastes, Fullham, Akimoto, Graves. Also CityToast gave a test read, and another fellow in 19 right before it went up.

This might be the most ridiculous thing I've ever written to completion in service of the Foundation wiki. I hope it makes you all as happy as it made me to write it.
